title: Vernonanthura rigiophylla
intro: Vernonanthura rigiophylla, also known as Candelilla, is a shrub-like plant native to the Americas, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing and skin protection. The bioactive constituents of Vernonanthura rigiophylla include vernolic acid, candelilla wax, and other triterpene esters, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Vernonanthura rigiophylla is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including creams, ointments, and salves, to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Vernonanthura rigiophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Brazil. Vernonanthura rigiophylla belongs to the genus Vernonanthura, which consists of flowering plants. The plant has been classified under the binomial nomenclature, a system used to identify species. This classification helps in understanding the plant's characteristics and its place in the botanical hierarchy.
